meta.name: Design Language Catalog
meta.note: Single source of truth. Prose reference files and scripts derive from this file.

styles.name: Skeuomorphism
styles.origin: Apple; term from Greek 'skeuos' (vessel). Popularized by early iOS/Mac OS X under Scott Forstall.
traits: Digital objects mimic real materials (leather, felt, brushed metal, paper)
Literal metaphors: notepad, bookshelf, calculator keys
Rich gradients, bevels, drop shadows and highlights for depth
Realistic affordances signaling how to interact
styles.example: Apple iOS 6 Notes (yellow legal pad), Find My Friends (leather), GarageBand wood.

styles.name: Neumorphism
styles.origin: Named after Alexander Plyuto's 2019 Dribbble concept; 'neumorphism' coined by Michał Malewicz.
traits: Extruded shapes that look pressed from the background (same base color)
Dual light+dark shadow pair creates soft embossing
styles.example: Alexander Plyuto's Skeuomorph 2.0 Dribbble concept; many fintech dashboard concepts.

styles.name: Claymorphism
traits: Puffy, inflated 3D shapes like modeling clay
Double inner shadow (light top-left, colored bottom-right) plus soft outer drop shadow
styles.example: Hype4 claymorphism generator; many 2022 illustration-heavy landing pages.

styles.name: Flat 2.0 / Semi-flat
styles.origin: Google Material (2014) and industry reaction to pure flat's usability problems.
traits: Flat foundation with subtle shadows and depth cues restored
styles.example: Google Material Design; most modern SaaS dashboards.

styles.name: Aurora UI
traits: Large soft blurred color blobs behind content
Dark backgrounds with luminous color washes
styles.example: Stripe landing pages; many AI startup sites (2023–2024).

styles.name: Mesh Gradient
styles.origin: Popularized by design tools (Mesh Gradient generators, Figma plugins).
traits: Multi-point smooth gradients that blend many hues
styles.example: Stripe backgrounds; Apple event key art.

styles.name: Soft 3D Illustration
styles.origin: Rise of Spline, Blender, and Dribbble 3D illustration trend.
styles.example: Spline showcase sites; Stripe/Vercel 3D hero scenes.

styles.name: Hyperrealism UI
styles.origin: High-water mark of skeuomorphic app design.
styles.example: Korg iMS-20 synth app; early podcast/voice-memo apps.

styles.name: Emboss / Deboss
styles.origin: Print letterpress heritage adapted to screen (text-shadow technique).
traits: Type looks stamped into or raised from the surface
styles.example: Letterpress text effects on textured web headers (~2010).

styles.name: Glassmorphism
styles.origin: Coined by Michał Malewicz (2020); echoes macOS Big Sur / Windows Acrylic.
traits: Frosted translucent panels via background blur
Thin light border + subtle inner highlight
styles.example: macOS Big Sur Control Center; Windows 11 Acrylic; countless 2020–2022 dashboards.

styles.name: Liquid Glass
styles.origin: Apple, announced WWDC June 2025; ships in iOS/iPadOS 26, macOS Tahoe, etc.
traits: Dynamic 'meta-material' that bends and refracts light like a lens
Specular edge highlights and real-time refraction of content behind
Fluid, springy motion; concentric rounded shapes matching hardware
styles.example: iOS 26 / macOS Tahoe system UI (tab bars, controls, sidebars).

styles.name: Windows Aero Glass
styles.origin: Microsoft Windows Vista (2006) and Windows 7 Aero.
traits: Translucent window chrome with Gaussian blur
Colored glass tint with reflective sheen
styles.example: Windows Vista / 7 title bars and taskbar.

styles.name: Acrylic (Fluent Material)
traits: Sits beneath interactive UI as a base layer
styles.example: Windows 11 flyouts, command bars, context menus.

styles.name: macOS Vibrancy / Translucency
traits: Sidebar/toolbar materials that sample and blur the desktop
Vibrant text/icons that adapt to backdrop

styles.name: Frosted Glass
styles.origin: Generic pattern popularized by iOS 7 blur layers.
traits: No strong refraction or edge lighting
styles.example: iOS Control Center; modal backdrops across the web.

styles.name: Brutalist Web Design
styles.origin: Named after architectural Brutalism (béton brut); web movement popularized via brutalistwebsites.com (Pascal Deville).
traits: Raw, unstyled or near-default HTML aesthetics
System fonts, default blue links, visible structure
High contrast, monospace, hard edges, no rounding
styles.example: brutalistwebsites.com; Bloomberg Businessweek features; Craigslist ethos.

styles.name: Neubrutalism
styles.origin: Rose with Gumroad's 2021 redesign; heavily used by Figma community.
traits: Thick solid black borders and hard offset drop shadows (no blur)
Loud, clashing saturated colors and flat fills
styles.example: Gumroad (2021 redesign); Figma marketing; many indie SaaS.

styles.name: Swiss Punk / New Wave
styles.origin: Wolfgang Weingart (Basel), spread by April Greiman and Dan Friedman in the US.
traits: Deconstructed Swiss grid; tilted, layered type
styles.example: April Greiman 'Does It Make Sense?' (1986); Weingart Typographische Monatsblätter.

styles.name: Grunge Design
styles.origin: David Carson (Ray Gun magazine); reaction to clean corporate design.
traits: Distressed textures, dirt, splatter, torn edges

styles.name: Raw / Default HTML
styles.origin: The literal default browser stylesheet; revived as an aesthetic statement.
traits: Times New Roman, default blue/purple links

styles.name: Deconstructivism (Graphic)
styles.origin: Cranbrook Academy; influenced by Derrida; Katherine McCoy, Edward Fella.
traits: Rejection of the grid; ambiguous reading paths
Overlap, rotation, and collision of elements

styles.name: Dadaist / Collage Web
styles.origin: Digital revival of Dada photomontage and chance-based layout.

styles.name: Flat Design
styles.origin: Microsoft Metro (2010) and Apple iOS 7 (2013) cemented it industry-wide.
traits: No shadows, gradients, or textures — pure flat color
Bold color blocking and generous whitespace
Typography and color carry the hierarchy
styles.example: iOS 7; Windows Phone; early Google/Microsoft rebrands.

styles.name: Metro / Microsoft Design Language
traits: Live Tiles grid; content over chrome

styles.name: Material Design (M2)
traits: Material metaphor: paper and ink with real elevation

styles.name: Material Design 3 / Material You
styles.origin: Google; Material You (2021), Material 3 Expressive (Google I/O 2025).
traits: Dynamic color extracted from user wallpaper (tonal palettes)
Design tokens as single source of truth; updated components

styles.name: Fluent Design 2 (Fluent 2)
styles.origin: Microsoft; Fluent (2017), Fluent 2 refresh (2021+ for Windows 11).
traits: Five fundamentals: light, depth, motion, material, scale
Mica tints long-lived surfaces with desktop color
Reveal highlight, subtle depth, rounded corners

extra.reveal-highlight: radial-gradient(circle at var(--x,50%) var(--y,50%), rgba(255,255,255,0.35), transparent 60%)

styles.name: Ant Design
traits: Blue primary, subtle shadows, 6px radius default
styles.example: Alibaba enterprise consoles; many admin panels.

styles.name: Apple HIG (pre-Liquid flat)
traits: SF Pro type, generous spacing, subtle depth

styles.name: Primer
traits: Mona Sans / system fonts, octicon iconography

styles.name: GOV.UK Design System
traits: No decoration; content and usability only

styles.name: Bauhaus
traits: Form follows function; geometric primitives (circle, square, triangle)
styles.example: Herbert Bayer's Universal typeface; Bauhaus exhibition posters.

styles.name: Swiss / International Typographic Style
styles.origin: Switzerland (Josef Müller-Brockmann, Armin Hofmann); Basel & Zurich schools.
traits: Mathematical grid systems and rigorous alignment

styles.name: De Stijl / Neoplasticism
traits: Only horizontals and verticals; thick black rules

styles.name: Constructivism
styles.origin: Russia/USSR (Alexander Rodchenko, El Lissitzky, Stenberg brothers).
traits: Red, black, cream; heavy sans/slab type
styles.example: Rodchenko posters; El Lissitzky 'Beat the Whites with the Red Wedge'.

styles.name: Suprematism
traits: Pure geometric abstraction floating in space
Limited palette, often on white ground

styles.name: Art Deco
styles.origin: Paris 1925 Exposition Internationale des Arts Décoratifs.
styles.example: Chrysler Building; The Great Gatsby posters; Cassandre travel posters.

styles.name: Art Nouveau
styles.origin: Europe (Alphonse Mucha, Hector Guimard, Gustav Klimt).
traits: Floral and botanical ornament, flowing hair
styles.example: Mucha's theatrical posters; Paris Métro entrances.

styles.name: Memphis Design
traits: Playful clashing colors and bold geometric shapes

styles.name: Postmodern Graphic Design
styles.origin: Reaction to Modernism (Wolfgang Weingart, April Greiman, David Carson).
styles.example: Emigre magazine; MTV identity; Cranbrook work.

styles.name: Pop Art
styles.origin: UK/US (Andy Warhol, Roy Lichtenstein, Eduardo Paolozzi).
traits: Bright saturated primary colors, bold outlines

styles.name: Op Art
traits: Illusions of motion, depth, and warping

styles.name: Dada
styles.origin: Zurich (Cabaret Voltaire), Berlin, NY (Tzara, Höch, Heartfield).
styles.example: Hannah Höch photomontages; Heartfield political montage.

styles.name: Futurism (Italian)
traits: Diagonal, chaotic type on the page

styles.name: Mid-Century Modern
styles.origin: Post-war US/Scandinavia (Charles & Ray Eames, Paul Rand era).
traits: Warm retro palette (mustard, teal, orange, brown)
styles.example: Eames furniture; Paul Rand IBM/Westinghouse logos; Saul Bass.

styles.name: Streamline Moderne
styles.origin: Late Art Deco offshoot, US industrial design.
traits: Aerodynamic curves and horizontal speed lines

styles.name: Vienna Secession
styles.origin: Vienna (Gustav Klimt, Koloman Moser, Josef Hoffmann).
traits: Geometric grids of squares married to ornament
Bridge between Art Nouveau and Deco
styles.example: Klimt Beethoven Frieze; Ver Sacrum journal.

styles.name: Punk Zine / Cut-and-Paste
styles.origin: UK/US punk subculture (Jamie Reid, Sex Pistols).
styles.example: Jamie Reid Sex Pistols 'God Save the Queen'; punk fanzines.

styles.name: WPA / Vintage Poster
styles.origin: US Works Progress Administration Federal Art Project.
styles.example: WPA national park posters; vintage airline/travel posters.

styles.name: Cyberpunk
styles.origin: Literary roots (Gibson 'Neuromancer' 1984); visual codex from Blade Runner (1982); revived by Cyberpunk 2077.
traits: Dense HUD/glitch overlays, mono type, scanlines
styles.example: Blade Runner; Cyberpunk 2077 UI; Ghost in the Shell.

styles.name: Solarpunk
styles.origin: Emerged online ~2008 as optimistic counter to cyberpunk.
traits: Lush greenery fused with clean technology
Warm sunlit palette: greens, gold, sky blue, terracotta

styles.name: Steampunk
traits: Exposed gears, cogs, rivets, pipes, gauges
Ornate Victorian serif type and filigree
styles.example: The Difference Engine; Wild Wild West; BioShock Infinite-adjacent.

styles.name: Dieselpunk
styles.origin: Term coined ~2001; aesthetic of 1920s–1940s diesel era.
styles.example: BioShock; Sky Captain and the World of Tomorrow.

styles.name: Atompunk
styles.example: Fallout series UI; The Jetsons; mid-century ads.

styles.name: Cassette Futurism
styles.origin: Fan term for retro-analog sci-fi tech aesthetics.
traits: CRT monitors, blinking LEDs, physical switches

styles.name: Biopunk
styles.origin: Offshoot of cyberpunk focused on biotechnology.
traits: Wet organic textures, flesh and fungus

styles.name: Y2K Futurism
traits: Metallic silver, icy blues, hot pink accents
styles.example: iMac G3; The Matrix marketing; early Windows XP era ads.

styles.name: Frutiger Aero
styles.origin: Retroactively named (after Frutiger typeface + Windows Aero); nature-meets-tech optimism.
traits: Nature imagery: water, bubbles, grass, blue skies
Aqua gradients, lens flares, clean tech
styles.example: Windows Vista/7; Wii/iOS-era wallpapers; Windows XP 'Bliss'.

styles.name: Vaporwave
aliases: aesthetic (A E S T H E T I C)
traits: Japanese katakana, marble, palm trees, grids
styles.example: Macintosh Plus 'Floral Shoppe'; vaporwave album art.

styles.name: Synthwave / Outrun
traits: Neon grid horizon and giant gradient sun
Chrome 80s type, palm trees, sports cars
styles.example: Kavinsky 'Nightcall'; Hotline Miami; Far Cry Blood Dragon.

styles.name: Raygun Gothic
styles.origin: Term popularized by William Gibson; pulp sci-fi + Googie.
traits: Sleek rockets, ray guns, chrome fins

styles.name: Googie
styles.example: LAX Theme Building; retro motel/diner signs.

styles.name: Space Age Pop
traits: Sleek white + primary pops, molded plastic
styles.example: 2001: A Space Odyssey interiors; Panton furniture.

styles.name: Vectordelia
styles.origin: Mid-2000s vector-art trend (part of Frutiger family).
styles.example: Mid-2000s tech ads; Windows Vista promo art.

styles.name: McBling
traits: Rhinestones, glitter, hot pink, gold bling
styles.example: Paris Hilton era; early Myspace glam.

styles.name: Laserwave / Darksynth
traits: Aggressive neon on black, horror tones

styles.name: Minimalism
styles.origin: Roots in minimal art (Donald Judd) + Dieter Rams; digital via Apple/Google.
styles.example: Apple product pages; Dieter Rams Braun; Google homepage.

styles.name: Maximalism
styles.origin: Reaction against flat minimalism; 'more is more'.
traits: Dense layering, clashing color and pattern
Big expressive type, many focal points

styles.name: Bento Grid
styles.origin: Named after Japanese bento boxes; popularized by Apple keynote slides & MagicUI.
traits: Modular rounded-rectangle cells of varied sizes
Each cell one idea/feature; dense but tidy
Consistent gaps, mixed media per cell
styles.example: Apple keynote feature slides; Vercel/Linear marketing; MagicUI.

styles.name: Organic / Biomorphic
styles.origin: Reaction to rigid grids; nature-derived forms.
styles.example: Wellness/beauty brand sites; many 2020s landing pages.

styles.name: Blobmorphism
styles.origin: Trend of amorphous blob shapes as UI/decoration.
traits: Irregular gooey blob backgrounds and masks
styles.example: Dribbble blob illustration sets; startup hero backgrounds.

styles.name: Scandinavian / Nordic
traits: Warm minimalism: light woods, soft neutrals

styles.name: Japandi
styles.origin: Hybrid of Japanese wabi-sabi and Scandinavian minimalism.

styles.name: Zen / Wabi-Sabi / Ma
styles.origin: Japanese aesthetics of imperfection (wabi-sabi) and negative space (ma).

styles.name: Editorial / Magazine
styles.origin: Magazine/print art direction adapted to screen.
styles.example: The New York Times; Kinfolk; editorial portfolio sites.

styles.name: Dark Mode
traits: Dark neutral backgrounds (#0d0d12-ish, not pure black)
Desaturated accent colors to avoid vibration
Elevation via lighter surfaces, not shadow
styles.example: System dark modes; Linear; developer tools.

styles.name: Monochrome / Grayscale
traits: Contrast and texture do the work

styles.name: Brutalist Minimalism
styles.origin: Cross of brutalism's honesty with minimalism's restraint.
traits: Big type, hairline rules, monospace accents

styles.name: Holographic / Iridescent
traits: Rainbow gradient that shifts by angle
Metallic foil sheen on dark or white

styles.name: Halftone
traits: Dot grids simulate tone via dot size
styles.example: Comic books; Roy Lichtenstein; retro posters.

styles.name: Risograph
styles.origin: Riso Kagaku duplicator; indie print revival.
traits: Limited bright spot-ink palette (fluoro pink, blue)
styles.example: Indie zines; risograph art prints; event posters.

styles.name: Low Poly
styles.origin: Stylized faceted 3D from real-time graphics constraints.
traits: Visible triangular facets and flat shading
styles.example: Indie game art; low-poly hero backgrounds.

styles.name: Pixel Art / 8-bit
styles.origin: Constraint of early raster displays, now deliberate style.
styles.example: Game Boy/NES games; Celeste; pixel-art portfolios.

styles.name: ASCII / Terminal / TUI
styles.origin: Text-only terminals; revived as retro/hacker aesthetic.
traits: Blinking cursor, ASCII art, command-line framing
styles.example: Old DOS/Unix terminals; modern TUIs; hacker movie UIs.

styles.name: CRT / Scanline / Retro Monitor
styles.example: Retro game emulators; Fallout terminals; VHS/CRT web filters.

styles.name: Glitch Art
styles.example: Glitch music visuals; cyberpunk UIs; error-art.

styles.name: VHS / Analog Video
traits: Tracking distortion, tape warble, timestamp overlay
styles.example: 80s home-video aesthetic; music videos; horror pastiche.

styles.name: Film Grain / Noise
styles.origin: Adding grain/noise to fight digital sterility.
traits: Subtle photographic grain over flat color
styles.example: A24 film sites; premium editorial (grainy gradients).

styles.name: Blueprint / Technical Drawing
styles.example: Product 'how it works' sections; engineering brand sites.

styles.name: Hand-Drawn / Sketch
styles.origin: Humanizing counter to sterile flat UI.

styles.name: Papercut / Paper Collage
styles.origin: Digital simulation of layered cut paper.
traits: Layered paper with soft realistic shadows

styles.name: Comic / Manga
traits: Speech bubbles, action lines, SFX lettering

styles.name: Engraving / Line Art
traits: Fine hatching and cross-hatching for tone
styles.example: Financial/whisky brand illustration; Ivan Ramen-style menus.

styles.name: Grainy Gradient
styles.origin: Adding grain to smooth gradients for depth.
traits: Smooth color gradient overlaid with fine grain

styles.name: Neon Sign / Tube
traits: Glowing tube strokes on dark ground

styles.name: Dark Academia
traits: Moody browns, oxblood, forest green, cream
Serif type, old books, candlelight, tweed

styles.name: Light Academia
traits: Warm creams, beige, soft gold, sage

styles.name: Cottagecore
styles.example: Cottagecore Tumblr; 'Anne with an E' moodboards.

styles.name: Goblincore
traits: Mushrooms, moss, frogs, snails, shiny trinkets

styles.name: Liminal Spaces
styles.origin: Internet fascination with empty transitional spaces.

styles.name: Brat
styles.example: Charli XCX 'Brat' album cover and campaign.

styles.name: Coquette
traits: Soft pinks, cream, lace, ribbon bows
styles.example: Coquette TikTok; Lana Del Rey-adjacent moodboards.

styles.name: Barbiecore
styles.origin: 2023 Barbie film revived hot-pink maximal femininity.

styles.name: Mallsoft
styles.origin: Vaporwave subgenre evoking dead shopping malls.

styles.name: Nostalgiacore
styles.origin: Broad nostalgia for the recent past.

styles.name: Clowncore
traits: Primary + rainbow clash, polka dots, stripes

styles.name: Witchcore / Witchy
traits: Deep purples, black, forest green, gold

styles.name: Scene / Emo
traits: Neon + black, hot pink, leopard, checkerboard
styles.example: Myspace scene profiles; 2000s emo band art.

styles.name: Tiki / Tropical
styles.example: Tiki bars; Trader Vic's; exotica album covers.

styles.name: Retro Americana / Diner
traits: Neon signage, script + bold slab type

styles.name: shadcn/ui (New York)
styles.origin: shadcn/ui (Radix + Tailwind) by shadcn; default of countless 2023+ apps.
traits: Neutral, refined, subtle borders and rings

styles.name: Vercel Geist
styles.origin: Vercel Geist design system + Geist typeface.

styles.name: Linear Style
traits: Dark UI with subtle purple/blue gradients

styles.name: Analytics Dashboard
traits: Card grids, KPI tiles, charts, tables
Neutral surfaces, one or two accent colors
styles.example: Stripe Dashboard; Vercel Analytics; admin panels.

styles.name: Modern Gradient SaaS
traits: Vibrant gradient hero + soft blurred blobs
Rounded cards, subtle shadows, gradient buttons

styles.name: Corporate Memphis
styles.origin: Named after Facebook's 'Alegria' illustration style; ubiquitous big-tech look.
traits: Flat vector people with tiny heads, long bendy limbs
Simple geometric bodies, bold flat colors

styles.name: Spatial / visionOS
traits: Translucent materials over the real world

styles.name: Ukiyo-e
traits: Flat areas of color with bold outlines
Nature, waves, Mt Fuji, everyday life

styles.name: Illuminated Manuscript
traits: Ornate drop caps and gold-leaf illumination
Rich reds, blues, gold on vellum
styles.example: Book of Kells; Très Riches Heures.

styles.name: Baroque / Rococo
styles.origin: European Baroque (17th c.) and Rococo (18th c.).

styles.name: Blackletter / Gothic Type
styles.example: The New York Times masthead; metal-band logos.

styles.name: Egyptian / Ancient Revival
styles.origin: Egyptomania after Napoleon's campaigns and Tutankhamun (1922).
traits: Hieroglyph and papyrus motifs, lotus, scarab
styles.example: Art Deco Egyptian revival; museum branding.

styles.name: Clockpunk
styles.origin: Punk genre set in Renaissance-era clockwork tech.

styles.name: Silkpunk
styles.origin: Coined by Ken Liu; East-Asian-inspired bio/mechanical tech.

styles.name: Decopunk
traits: Polished Art Deco chrome and glamour

styles.name: Tidalpunk
traits: Ocean tech: wave/tidal energy, kelp, coral

styles.name: Airbrush / 70s-80s Illustration
traits: Smooth gradient shading and soft glows

styles.name: Terrazzo
traits: Speckled chip pattern on solid ground
Soft pastel or bold color chips

styles.name: Marble / Stone
styles.origin: Natural stone as luxury material reference.

styles.name: Lo-fi Wireframe
styles.origin: Wireframe/mockup look used as a finished style.
traits: Grey boxes, placeholder lines, X'd images

styles.name: Stipple / Pointillism
traits: Tone built from dots of varying density

styles.name: Gorpcore
styles.origin: Fashion trend elevating technical outdoor gear ('GORP').
traits: Technical outdoor apparel as everyday style

styles.name: Quiet Luxury / Old Money
traits: Understated neutral tones: camel, cream, navy

styles.name: Cluttercore
styles.origin: Cozy maximalist embrace of visible stuff.
traits: Warm eclectic clutter, no empty space

styles.name: VSCO Girl
styles.origin: Summer-2019 teen aesthetic (named after VSCO app).

styles.name: Typography-Driven
traits: Oversized expressive typography as the design

styles.name: Samsung One UI
traits: Large rounded content zones, reachable UI
